name: smart_source_reader
description: Read source files intelligently: list function/class definitions, grep for patterns, show line ranges, or read whole files. Supports batch mode to read multiple file specs in one call. All output includes line numbers.
when_to_use: Use when you need to understand source code structure. Replaces 2-3 separate tool calls (grep for functions, read specific lines) with a single script call. Batch mode eliminates 5-10 sequential read steps by reading multiple files at once. Particularly useful when you need to inspect multiple specific line ranges across different files.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: File path, or comma-separated file:line-range specs for batch mode, or glob pattern for batch reading all matching files

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action: 'grep' (search single pattern), 'lines' (show line range), 'functions' (list def/class lines), 'all' (functions + first 200 lines, default), 'batch' (read multiple file specs at once)

parameters.name: pattern_or_line_start
parameters.description: For 'grep' action: pattern to search (default: 'def '). For 'lines' and 'all' actions: starting line number (default: 1)

parameters.name: line_end
parameters.description: Ending line number (used with lines action, default: 200 or end of file)

examples.expected: All function/method definitions in the file with line numbers.
All lines containing 'contact' with line numbers.
Multiple files read in one call.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-3 separate tool calls (one to grep for functions, another to read specific lines) with a single script call. Batch mode eliminates 5-10 sequential read steps by reading multiple files at once. The line-numbered output makes it easy to reference specific sections for subsequent edits.
